title: Steve Kerr, Warriors Hold Two-Hour Meeting With No Resolution On Coaching Future
summary: Golden State Warriors head coach Steve Kerr met with controlling owner Joe Lacob and general manager Mike Dunleavy for two hours Monday, yet the two-week-old question of whether Kerr will return next season remains unresolved, according to ESPN sources. The parties plan to meet again next week. Both sides characterized the session as constructive. Kerr is described by those close to him as genuinely conflicted about his own decision, and will depart on a previously scheduled golf trip before talks resume. "It's April," one source said. "We don't need to rush." The draft lottery on May 10 represents the first significant offseason milestone. Golden State holds the 11th slot, carrying a 9.4% chance of moving into the top four and a 77.6% chance of remaining at 11. Central to Kerr's deliberations is whether the franchise can meaningfully upgrade its roster this summer. Sources indicate there is broad agreement that Kerr remains a strong fit leading a legitimate playoff contender built around Stephen Curry and Draymond Green, but not necessarily the right choice to spearhead a full organizational rebuild. "I still love coaching, but I get it," Kerr said the night Golden State was eliminated. "These jobs all have an expiration date. There is a run that happens, and when the run ends, sometimes it's time for new blood and new ideas." Team sources insist the deliberations are purely a basketball decision, centered on Kerr's coaching appetite, potential offensive philosophy adjustments and roster-building confidence heading into a competitive Western Conference. Warriors management has not initiated a coaching search as that process would only begin if Kerr chooses to step away.

title: Caleb Wilson Increasingly Viewed By NBA Scouts In Same Tier As Dybantsa, Peterson, Boozer
summary: North Carolina forward Caleb Wilson is slotted at fourth overall in Sam Vecenie's latest NBA mock draft for The Athletic, buoyed by strong head-to-head performances against his top rivals despite missing the final month of the season with a broken right thumb and broken left hand. Before the injuries, Wilson averaged 19.8 points, 9.4 rebounds, 2.7 assists, 1.5 steals and 1.4 blocks per game. Scouts are increasingly placing him alongside AJ Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson and Cameron Boozer as a legitimate top-three candidate in the class ahead of one or more of the other three prospects at the top of draft boards. Wilson's physical profile drives the evaluation. His combination of body strength, leaping ability and low-center-of-gravity driving technique makes him a punishing finisher at the rim. Vecenie draws a direct comparison to Pascal Siakam, a multiple All-NBA selection. Concerns exist, however. Wilson attempted just 27 three-pointers all season, converting only seven, though his mechanics from extended midrange range drew positive reviews. Defensively, his block and steal totals are impressive, but scouts note he lacks the lateral quickness expected of an athlete his size and struggles with off-ball rotations and reaction time. Wilson's most compelling asset, evaluators say, remains his relentless motor and physical aggression, qualities that proved reliable throughout the season and few prospects at any level consistently demonstrate.

summary: DeAndre Jordan has won the 2025-26 Twyman-Stokes Teammate of the Year , beating out Jrue Holiday, Jeff Green and Garrett Temple. Holiday has won the award three times in his career. Jordan signed with the New Orleans Pelicans this season after spending the previous three with the Denver Nuggets. The award recognizes the player deemed the league's best teammate, based on selfless play, leadership on and off the court as a mentor and role model to other NBA players, and commitment to the team. League executives selected 12 finalists while current NBA players then selected the winner from that group. Other nominees include Pat Connaughton, Jalen Brunson, Jayson Tatum, De'Aaron Fox, Duncan Robinson, Jaylin Williams, Desmond Bane and Marcus Smart. Stephen Curry won the award last season, while Mike Conley is a three-time winner.

title: Every NBA Champion Since 2015 Has Won First Round In Five Or Fewer Games
summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder swept the Phoenix Suns in the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs as they look to extend a remarkable 11-year pattern in which every NBA champion has advanced past the first round in five or fewer games. The streak dates to the Golden State Warriors' title run in 2015. Oklahoma City also swept the Memphis Grizzlies in last season's first round en route to the championship, making the Thunder just the sixth team to sweep the opening round and go on to win the title since the NBA expanded to a best-of-seven first round format in 2003. NBA Champions With First Round Sweep • 2013: Miami Heat (Bucks) • 2015: Golden State Warriors (Pelicans) • 2016: Cleveland Cavaliers (Pistons) • 2017: Golden State Warriors (Blazers) • 2021: Milwaukee Bucks (Heat) • 2025: Oklahoma City Thunder (Grizzlies) The San Antonio Spurs have the second best odds of winning the NBA championship and eliminated the Portland Trail Blazers in their first round series in five games, which keeps them within the historical pattern. Since 2003, seventeen champions have exited the first round with one loss or fewer, representing a 74 percent success rate. Only the 2008 Boston Celtics and 2014 Spurs survived seven-game first-round series before ultimately winning the title. Historically, champions exiting the first round with minimal resistance have proven the rule rather than the exception as they immediately exert their dominance at the start of the playoffs. title: AJ Dybantsa Converts Nike NIL Deal Into Pro Contract
summary: BYU freshman AJ Dybantsa is converting his existing Nike NIL agreement into a professional endorsement contract as he prepares for the 2026 NBA Draft, he confirmed to ESPN. The 6-foot-9 forward is widely projected as a top pick in June . "It means everything," Dybantsa said. "They've been with me since Day 1. It's been a great partnership and we're looking forward to keep building for the long term." Nike also unveiled a personal logo for Dybantsa, a star composed of his initials, tied to the new deal. The design references his childhood nickname, "Star Boy." Dybantsa led the nation in scoring during his lone college season, averaging 25.5 points per game on 51% shooting, with 6.8 rebounds and 3.7 assists at BYU. He has been training in Los Angeles alongside former Boston Celtics guard Isaiah Thomas and trainer Zack Gonzales. "Ever since the rankings came out in 9th grade, I've been No. 1 [in my class]," Dybantsa told ESPN. "I haven't been nothing lower. So, it's definitely a goal of mine to go No. 1." The Boston native is also adjusting to the extended NBA three-point line after shooting 33.1% from deep in college. He attended Game 2 of the Lakers-Rockets playoff series to observe the professional game firsthand. BYU's strength staff compared Dybantsa's athletic metrics directly to current NBA players, while head coach Kevin Young, a former Philadelphia 76ers and Phoenix Suns assistant, emphasized pro-style schemes throughout the season. Beyond basketball, Dybantsa told ESPN he intends to establish charitable roots in whichever city selects him, drawing inspiration from his father's philanthropic work in the Republic of the Congo. He also made his immediate competitive ambitions clear. "There's so many greats that have won Rookie of the Year ," he said. "I don't want to not win Rookie of the Year."

summary: The Detroit Pistons are looking towards next season, while still competing in the 2026 NBA Playoffs. The Pistons reportedly are close to agreeing with veteran forward Isaac Bonga on a deal that would bring him back to the NBA. Bonga is currently playing for Partizan in Serbia. The 6-foot-8 forward last played in the NBA in 2022 with the Toronto Raptors. Prior to that, Bonga spent one season with the Los Angeles Lakers and two seasons with the Washington Wizards. Despite showing potential in the NBA, the German national never broke through. Over the last four seasons, split between Bayern Munich in Germany and Partizan, Bonga has broken through. This season he averaged 10.2 points, 5.5 rebounds on 51/37/83 shooting splits. The 26-year-old has also become a valuable member of the German national team. Bonga has appeared in two Olympics, the FIBA World Cup and EuroBasket, as well as qualifiers for the aforementioned tournaments for Germany. Several NBA teams reached out to Partizan about Bonga's availability midseason, but the Serbian club held off interest. Bonga has a buyout of $875,000 this offseason that would allow him to join an NBA team.

title: LeBron James Addresses Michael Jordan GOAT Debate, Finals Record In Wide-Ranging Interview
summary: LeBron James, 41, spoke candidly about his relationship with Michael Jordan's legacy in a series of ESPN interviews conducted throughout the season. The Los Angeles Lakers star addressed the GOAT debate, stylistic differences between the two players, and his 4-6 Finals record. The interviews, conducted by ESPN's Dave McMenamin across eight cities over the course of the year, capture James near the conclusion of his NBA career. "I hope I made him proud at least, wearing that No. 23," James told McMenamin. James, listed at 6-foot-9 and 250 pounds, was direct about the stylistic contrast between himself and Jordan, who played at 6-6 and 200 pounds. "I never have compared myself to MJ because our games are totally different," James said. "I have been a point-forward/forward-point my whole life. I have always looked for the pass. MJ kind of looked for the shot. Not kind of, he did. He looked for the shot. There are a lot of things where I would say my game is a lot different and a little better than his, but s---, he was f---ing great. We're both great. We're both great basketball players." James credited Jordan as a formative influence during his childhood in Akron, Ohio. "I never thought that I could be him, but I dreamed of the opportunity to live in the shoes that he lived in," James said. "I dreamed of being able to be in a big game and hit a game winner as the clock went down. I dreamed of having my own sneaker. I dreamed of flying through the air like him. I dreamed of people screaming my name. Everything that he did. I needed inspiration from certain people in music and in sports during my upbringing. I needed that." On his Finals record, James pushed back against critics who use it to diminish his career. "I am pissed off that I didn't have a better winning percentage individually in the Finals, but for people to try to turn it into a negative, it doesn't bother me like it did when I was younger," James said.

title: Jalen Brunson Scores 39 Points As Knicks Take 3-2 Series Lead
summary: The New York Knicks defeated the Atlanta Hawks 126-97 at Madison Square Garden on Tuesday night to take a 3-2 series lead. Jalen Brunson finished with 39 points, including scoring 17 in the fourth quarter. "The ball went in, but just happy to we were able to keep the lead," Brunson said. "They're a team that's capable of going on runs, as you know, from early in the series. But I liked the way we played in the fourth and kept the lead." OG Anunoby added 17 points and 10 rebounds, with Karl-Anthony Towns getting 16 points and 11 rebounds. Since falling behind 2-1 in the series, the Knicks have won the last two games by an average of 22.5 points. The team also limited CJ McCollum to six points. "It's been a multitude of things. We picked it up as a unit," Brunson said of the team's defense. "They've also gotten a lot of looks and we were lucky they were missing. I think us being on the same page, both sides of the ball, was a factor." Game 6 takes place on Thursday with the Knicks favored by 2.5 points to close out the series .

title: Joel Embiid On Scoring 33 Points In Game 5 Win: 'You Do Whatever It Takes'
summary: The Philadelphia 76ers defeated the Boston Celtics 113-97 in Game 5 to stave off elimination on Tuesday. Joel Embiid finished with 33 points, with Tyrese Maxey adding 25 points and 10 rebounds. "Obviously you don't want to go home, so you do whatever it takes," said Embiid. "I thought defensively we did a much better job, especially not overhelping as we did last game. And yeah, then offensively it becomes a little easier when you're making shots. We definitely made more shots than we did last game." The Sixers were down 86-85 to start the fourth before outscoring the Celtics 28-11 in the quarter. Embiid added that he focused on scoring in the paint as the game progressed. "To start the game, it was a little bit more of outside than in," said Embiid. "And then in the second half, it was both. But then again, inside, I feel pretty good about my chances of going one-on-one against anybody in this league, and I don't think that I can be stopped. So I just took advantage of what they were giving me." Jayson Tatum lead the Celtics with 24 points and 16 rebounds. Under Joe Mazzulla, the Celtics are 19-12 at home and 17-7 on the road in the playoffs. "I don't know," said Payton Pritchard on team's struggles at home. "We're going to have to figure that out. Can't really put my finger on it, but honestly, it feels like we've been better on the road. We don't want that to necessarily be the case. We want to be dominant at home and on the road. Definitely got to get that fixed." With the series at 3-2, Game 6 is at Philadelphia on Thursday with the Celtics favored by 5.5 points .

title: NCAA Tournament Expansion To 76 Teams On Track For 2026-27
summary: The NCAA has launched the final steps toward expanding both the men's and women's basketball tournaments from 68 to 76 teams, sources told ESPN. The move is expected to be formalized within weeks and would take effect for the upcoming 2026-27 season, with a mid-May announcement possible. Remaining committee approvals are considered procedural at this stage. "They have what they need to move forward," one source said. NCAA officials met with men's tournament media partners last week to finalize contract terms, though agreements had not yet been signed as of Tuesday. Committee approvals, spanning the men's and women's basketball committees, oversight committees, the Division I cabinet and board of governors, require signed contracts before formal votes can occur. The NCAA cautioned in a statement that "no final recommendations or decisions have been made at this time." Power conferences have been the primary force behind the expansion, seeking additional at-large berths as their membership has grown throughout the current media rights cycle. Financial gain is a secondary factor, with sources describing the expected revenue increase as delivering only "a modest financial upside" after covering added logistics costs and additional NCAA tournament units. Under the proposed men's tournament structure, eight additional at-large bids would be introduced. The current First Four format, four games involving eight teams in Dayton, Ohio, would grow to 12 games across 24 teams at two sites. Dayton is expected to retain one of those locations, with a second site west of the Eastern time zone still to be determined. The expanded opening round would feature 24 teams competing on the Tuesday and Wednesday before the traditional 64-team bracket tips off Thursday, largely unchanged in structure, though more at-large qualifiers would be required to play in the preliminary round.

title: Consensus No. 1 2027 NBA Draft Prospect Tyran Stokes Commits To Kansas Over Kentucky
summary: Tyran Stokes, the nation's top-ranked senior and consensus favorite to be the No. 1 pick in the 2027 NBA Draft, has committed to Kansas, he announced Tuesday. The 6-foot-7 small forward from Rainier Beach High School in Seattle chose the Jayhawks over Kentucky following an intense months-long recruiting battle. The commitment gives Bill Self a potential No. 1 overall pick for the second straight season, following Darryn Peterson's year in Lawrence. Kansas now holds the No. 2-ranked recruiting class in the country, with Stokes joining five-star point guard Taylen Kinney in the 2026 class. Kentucky appeared to be the clear frontrunner entering the fall, but Kansas surged as the college season progressed. A late official visit to Lexington allowed the Wildcats to close the gap before the Jayhawks ultimately held firm against Mark Pope's program. Stokes, originally from Louisville, led Rainier Beach to a state championship this past season, including a 63-point performance on senior night with assistant coaches from both Kansas and Kentucky in attendance. The forward possesses a rare combination of scoring, rebounding and playmaking. On the Nike EYBL circuit last summer, he averaged 22.4 points, 10.2 rebounds and 3.7 assists while shooting 51% from the field and drawing 10.4 free throw attempts per game. His playmaking approach draws comparisons to Portland Trail Blazers forward Deni Avdija. Stokes has earned three gold medals with USA Basketball, most recently at last summer's FIBA U19 Basketball World Cup. He has been regarded as the top player in his class for the better part of two years, collecting power-conference scholarship offers before his freshman season at Prolific Prep in California had even started. Self enters next season with his most decorated recruiting class in years, bolstered further by transfer portal additions Leroy Blyden Jr., Keanu Dawes and Christian Reeves.

title: NBA Presents '3-2-1 Lottery' Anti-Tanking Reform To GMs; Owners Vote May 28
summary: The NBA has presented a sweeping draft overhaul to its 30 general managers called the "3-2-1 lottery," a reform that would expand the draft lottery from 14 to 16 teams and introduce a relegation zone penalizing the league's three worst records, sources told ESPN. The proposal heads to an owners' vote on May 28, with the new system set to take effect beginning with the 2027 draft. The framework has drawn majority support from teams following several weeks of meetings with the board of governors, competition committee and general managers. Minor adjustments remain possible, but the core structure is largely settled, according to sources. Under the proposal, non-playoff teams outside the bottom three, slots four through 10, would each receive three lottery balls. The three worst teams would receive just two balls each but are guaranteed no worse than the 12th pick. The remaining 13 lottery teams could fall as low as 16th. The 9th and 10th play-in seeds receive two lottery balls each, while losers of the 7-8 play-in games receive one ball apiece. Additional restrictions include a ban on consecutive No. 1 pick wins , a prohibition on winning three straight top-five picks and an elimination of pick protections in the 12-15 range. The plan includes a sunset provision expiring after the 2029 draft, aligning with the NBA's current collective bargaining agreement. The league would also gain expanded authority to reduce lottery odds or adjust draft positions as a disciplinary measure against tanking. Adam Silver identified tanking as his foremost priority following a board of governors meeting in March. "I do think ultimately this is a decision that needs to be made at the ownership level," Silver said in March. "It has business implications, has basketball implications, has integrity implications for the league. "So, it's one that we take very seriously, and we are going to fix it. Full stop."

title: Steve Kerr's Future With Warriors Could Come Down To Negotiating More Than Sentimentality
summary: Steve Kerr and the Golden State Warriors are expected to soon meet to discuss whether he will continue his tenure as head coach. The situation is described as being far more complicated than whether or not Kerr wants to continue coaching as there is a financial element to it and also questions of whether he will be required to make coaching changes in terms of both his staff and his style. "I think Steve Kerr has sent the message out that he would be very interested in returning to coach the Warriors," said Brian Windhorst on Yahoo Sports Daily. "But what I think people have misunderstood about this [is that] this is not a straight emotional decision, like, 'Do I have it within me to coach the Warriors or not?' "There's not a contract sitting on the table. It's not like the Warriors said, 'Hey man, sign this at any time, whenever you feel like it.' They did not have contract extension discussions. They called those off. Steve called those off. "He is making the highest salary amongst all NBA coaches at $17 million, and the team has not performed at, you know, that's a championship-competition-level salary. This team is not a championship contender right now. "The other thing, and my colleagues Ramona Shelburne and Anthony Slater have reported on this, there is some desire within the organization for Steve Kerr to potentially change his coaching staff, and some desire for Steve Kerr to change some strategy, particularly offensively. "Now, that is not a controversial statement. When a team that is this expensive and this veteran underachieves and misses the playoffs, to say that you want potential changes is not a shocking thing. "But the question is: is Kerr willing to make those changes? Is Kerr willing to coach for a different salary next year? These are the real questions. This is where the rubber meets the road. He obviously has to ask himself the question of whether he can invest himself in the job, but he's indicated that he'd like to. "This might come down to a negotiating thing. It might not be sentimental at all. This might be good old-fashioned negotiating. Steve Kerr can leave the sideline and walk right into a broadcasting job, in a selection of places, for big money and an easier life. So his leverage is there that he has that out there. "This is not the framing that I think people have in mind that it's just, you know, does he wipe away the tears and sign the contract? There are other things to be dealt with here." The Warriors hired Kerr away from his broadcasting role with TNT in 2014 to replace Mark Jackson and they went on to reach five straight Finals and win three championships with a fourth title coming in 2022. The Warriors have only won two playoff series over the past four seasons .

title: Brad Stevens Wins NBA Executive Of The Year For Second Time
summary: Boston Celtics President of Basketball Operations Brad Stevens has been named the 2025-26 NBA Basketball Executive of the Year. Stevens claimed the award for the second time, having previously won it in 2023-24. Stevens earned 69 total points in voting, collecting 11 first-place votes, four second-place votes and two third-place votes. Saleh Onsi of the Atlanta Hawks finished second with 41 points, followed by Trajan Langdon of the Detroit Pistons at 40 and Jeff Peterson of the Charlotte Hornets at 37. The Celtics finished 56-26 this regular season, the second-best record in the Eastern Conference. Boston secured a top-two playoff seed for the fifth consecutive year, matching Stevens' five full seasons in his current front office role. Stevens becomes the 12th executive to win the award more than once since it was first presented in 1972-73. His roster construction guided Boston through a competitive Eastern Conference without sacrificing depth or continuity. Before moving into the front office, Stevens spent eight seasons as Boston's head coach. Across his 13-year tenure with the franchise in both roles, the Celtics have reached the playoffs 12 times.

title: Darryn Peterson's Work Ethic Not In Question As NBA Teams Focus On Health
summary: Front office sources across the NBA have offered largely positive assessments of Kansas guard Darryn Peterson, describing him as a dedicated worker passionate about his craft. Health, not talent or character, remains the central concern as teams evaluate his No. 1 overall candidacy ahead of the May 10 combine in Chicago. Peterson averaged 20.2 points, 4.2 rebounds and 1.6 assists in 24 games this season before a preseason full-body cramping episode disrupted his year both physically and mentally. The incident limited his availability and prompted deeper scrutiny from franchises holding top lottery picks. Many executives viewed Peterson as a straightforward No. 1 selection entering the fall. His elite shooting and offensive skill set remain highly regarded. Teams now want confirmation from his medical evaluation at the combine that they can count on the best version of him at the next level. BYU's AJ Dybantsa is currently projected as the probable top pick , with Peterson slotted second to the Indiana Pacers in the latest ESPN mock. However, a clean medical report could reignite direct competition between the two for the top spot. The Indiana Pacers hold a 52.1% chance of retaining a top-four pick. If the selection falls outside that range, it transfers to the Los Angeles Clippers. title: Nuggets Force Game 6 Behind Jokic's Triple-Double; McDaniels: 'We Going To Win The Next One'
summary: Nikola Jokic delivered a 27-point, 16-assist, 12-rebound triple-double Monday to keep the Denver Nuggets' season alive, as they defeated the Minnesota Timberwolves 125-113 in Game 5. The series shifts to Minneapolis for Game 6 on Thursday with the Nuggets favored by 6.5 points . Oddsmakers now give the Nuggets a 61 percent probability of winning the series and completing the 3-1 comeback. Denver entered as an 11-point favorite against a Timberwolves squad already missing injured stars Anthony Edwards and Donte DiVincenzo. Despite the favorable matchup, the Nuggets had dropped three straight games in increasingly deflating fashion before Monday's rebound. The Nuggets completed two 3-1 series comebacks in 2020 and also won three straight against the Wolves in the 2024 Western Conference semifinals before losing the final two. "Just an embarrassing first four games of the series," Christian Braun said. Jokic's supporting cast rose to the occasion alongside him. Spencer Jones scored 20 points on four three-pointers in place of the injured Aaron Gordon, while Cam Johnson, acquired in an offseason deal that sent Michael Porter Jr. to the Brooklyn Nets, posted 18 points. The Nuggets forced 25 turnovers and converted them into 35 points. Denver also shot 38% from three-point range after a collective 28.5% mark through the first four games. "Jok is Jok," said Nuggets coach David Adelman. "These [small] sample size games and the freakouts when he doesn't play well. You know he's going to play well. Then his 16 assists. Assists are hard to get unless you make shots." Timberwolves forward Jaden McDaniels struggled in his most difficult outing of the series, committing four turnovers and missing all three three-point attempts after drawing early foul trouble. McDaniels had sparked a late-game skirmish in Game 4 and publicly called several Nuggets players "bad defenders" following Game 2. "Nothing dirty," McDaniels said. "It's all fun to me. I feed into it. We just got to get the job done next game, and there's no more talking." Gordon and Peyton Watson remain questionable for Denver ahead of Game 6. "We just ended up losing today," McDaniels said. "But we going to win the next one."

title: Devin Booker Says Thunder Have 'All The Answers To The Test' Following Sweep
summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder eliminated the Phoenix Suns in a first-round sweep Monday, winning Game 4 by a 131-122 final. Oklahoma City has now won 12 consecutive first-round games , sweeping the opening round in each of the past three postseasons. The Thunder advance to face either the Houston Rockets or Los Angeles Lakers in the Western Conference semifinals. The Thunder are currently -115 favorites to win the 2026 NBA championship , which puts their probability at 53.5 percent Shai Gilgeous-Alexander posted 31 points and eight assists in the clincher, averaging 33.8 points and 8.0 assists across the series. Chet Holmgren added 24 points and Ajay Mitchell contributed 22, as six Thunder players finished in double figures. "He's the MVP of the league, and he's playing the best basketball in the league for the past two years," Devin Booker said of Gilgeous-Alexander. "That's mixed with guys that know their roles, some seasoned vets, and they're relentless. With that chemistry, the communication, it's on 100%. And when the bench comes in, they get better sometimes. It's like reinforcement, not subs. They have all the answers to the test." Phoenix finished 45-37 under rookie head coach Jordan Ott, a nine-win improvement from last season, despite widespread expectations of another lottery finish. The Suns had previously shed Kevin Durant via trade and waived Bradley Beal ahead of the rebuild. "We're supposed to be a laughingstock, a losing team, from all media and whoever else said it," said Dillon Brooks. "We proved a lot of people wrong, and that's just because we had heart. We came together, we played with each other and we believed in each other. So, even though it sucks that you get swept in the playoffs by a great team, it doesn't go against our season and what we're building here."
